title: 10 best herbal teas for hangover
intro_desc: Herbal teas for hangover are infusions made from various plants and herbs that help alleviate the symptoms of a hangover. These teas typically contain ingredients like ginger, lemon balm, chamomile, and milk thistle, which are known for their anti-inflammatory, calming, and liver-detoxifying properties. By drinking these teas, individuals can reduce the severity of hangover symptoms such as headaches, nausea, and dehydration. For example, ginger tea can help soothe an upset stomach, while chamomile tea can promote relaxation and improve sleep quality. By incorporating these teas into their routine, individuals can improve their overall well-being and reduce the negative effects of alcohol consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood teas helps with hangover because of its potent antibacterial and antifungal properties, which help to combat the toxins that accumulate in the body after excessive drinking. The tea's bitter compounds also stimulate digestive enzymes, aiding in the breakdown and elimination of ethanol and other harmful substances. Additionally, wormwood's anti-inflammatory effects may help alleviate the discomfort and fatigue associated with hangovers.
remedy_properties: Aromatic compounds: Wormwood tea contains aromatic compounds such as thujone, which can help to calm nausea and alleviate stomach cramps associated with hangover symptoms by reducing inflammation and soothing the digestive system.

Antioxidants: Wormwood tea is rich in antioxidants like flavonoids and terpenes, which help to neutralize free radicals in the body, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can exacerbate hangover symptoms like fatigue, headache, and brain fog.

Digestive stimulants: The essential oils and alkaloids present in wormwood tea, such as aristolochic acid, have been shown to stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut motility, helping to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with hangovers by promoting healthy digestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bitter compounds that help stimulate digestion and alleviate nausea associated with hangovers.
Bark: Containing tannins, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe stomach discomfort caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality artemisia absinthium leaves and stems.
Dry or store fresh harvested material properly.
Measure and combine ingredients according to dosage requirements.
Prepare tea by steeping ingredients in hot water.
Strain and serve immediately or store cooled tea for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with hangover because it contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms such as headaches, fatigue, and nausea. The diuretic properties of dandelion also help to flush out excess toxins and fluids from the body, reducing the risk of dehydration and promoting overall recovery. Additionally, the bitter flavor of dandelion tea can help stimulate digestive enzymes and relieve stomach upset, making it a natural remedy for hangover relief.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Dandelion tea helps to increase urine production, which can aid in flushing out toxins and excess sodium from the body that may have accumulated during excessive drinking.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by alcohol consumption, alleviating symptoms such as headaches and nausea.
Gastroprotective properties: Dandelion tea has been shown to soothe the digestive tract and protect it from damage caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which can lead to stomach upset and cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots are valued for their diuretic properties, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms such as dehydration and bloating.
Seeds: The seeds of the dandelion plant contain inulin, a natural prebiotic that can help soothe digestive issues common after excessive drinking.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried or fresh dandelion roots and leaves for optimal flavor and benefits.
Measure one teaspoon of dried dandelion root or two teaspoons of fresh dandelion leaves per eight ounces water.
Heat filtered water to boiling point then reduce heat and steep mixture for 5-7 minutes.
Strain tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids and enjoy hot or chilled as desired.
Store leftover tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days for later consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile teas helps with hangover because its soothing properties can calm an upset stomach, reducing nausea and inflammation. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, apigenin and bisabolol, also help to alleviate headaches and fatigue associated with a hangover. Additionally, the calming effects of chamomile can promote relaxation and reduce stress, making it easier to recover from a night out. By sipping on this gentle tea, you can ease your way back to feeling refreshed and revitalized after a big night.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: German Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, can help reduce inflammation in the body caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which may contribute to hangover symptoms.

Soothing properties: The flavonoids and terpenoids present in German Chamomile tea have a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing nausea, vomiting, and stomach discomfort often experienced during hangovers.

Antioxidant activity: German Chamomile's antioxidants can help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by free radicals generated by excessive alcohol consumption, which may contribute to hangover symptoms like fatigue, headaches, and mental fogginess.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin and other flavonoids, roots help calm digestive issues and reduce inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
Seeds: Containing a high concentration of azulene, seeds possess anti-inflammatory properties that aid in soothing nausea and stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried matricaria chamomilla flowers or fresh blooms with no signs of wilting.
Measure one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers or two teaspoons of fresh chamomilla blooms for every 8 ounces water.
Bring filtered water to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 5-7 minutes to steep the herbal tea.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids and sediment.
Enjoy warm or at room temperature within 30-40 minutes of preparation to maximize potency and flavor.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with hangover because its soothing properties can ease nausea and digestive issues that often accompany a night of heavy drinking. The menthol in peppermint also helps to stimulate digestion, reducing bloating and cramps, while its calming effects can ease headaches and promote relaxation. Additionally, peppermint's natural anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate dehydration-related discomforts, such as dry mouth and fatigue. Overall, a warm cup of herbal peppermint tea can provide a gentle and comforting remedy for hangover symptoms.
remedy_properties: Digestive Aid: Peppermint tea helps to soothe the stomach and alleviate nausea, which is often a debilitating symptom of hangovers.
Pain Relief: The menthol in peppermint oil has natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce headaches and muscle pains associated with hangovers.

Anti-Constipation: Peppermint tea's carminative properties can help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, preventing or relieving constipation, which is a common side effect of excessive alcohol consumption.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant are known for their calming properties, which can help soothe an upset stomach and reduce nausea associated with hangovers.
Bark: The bark of the peppermint plant contains flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate headaches and fatigue caused by excessive drinking.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or store dried peppermint herbs.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves or three to five sprigs of fresh peppermint per cup.
Heat filtered water in a teapot to a simmering temperature between 195°f and 205°f.
Steep the peppermint leaves in the hot water for seven to ten minutes to allow flavors to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup and enjoy immediately, or store it in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with hangover because it contains rosmarinic acid, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate headaches and reduce nausea. The tea's calming effects also ease irritability and anxiety, common symptoms of a hangover. Additionally, lemon balm's soothing properties can help relax digestive muscles, reducing the discomfort and bloating often associated with overindulgence.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lemon balm tea's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which can contribute to hangover symptoms.

Digestive Soothing Properties: The rosmarinic acid present in lemon balm tea has a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to alleviate nausea, stomach cramps, and other gastrointestinal issues commonly associated with hangovers.

Anxiolytic (Mood-Calming) Effects: Lemon balm tea's essential oils, particularly citral, have a tranquilizing effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce anxiety, stress, and irritability that often accompany hangover symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots are used to make tea due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe an upset stomach and reduce headache severity.
Flowers: The flowers of the lemon balm plant contain essential oils that provide a gentle, soothing effect on the digestive system, helping to ease nausea and vomiting associated with hangovers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lemon balm leaves or purchase dried melissa officinalis flowers from a reputable source.
Rinse the fresh lemon balm leaves with cool water to remove any dirt or debris and pat them dry.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried lemon balm flowers or two tablespoons of fresh leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the herbal mixture in boiling water for five to seven minutes, allowing the flavors to meld together.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids, serving hot or cold as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with hangover because it aids in digestion and alleviates nausea, two common complaints after a night of heavy drinking. The tea's natural anti-inflammatory properties can also help to soothe an upset stomach and reduce bloating, allowing the body to recover more efficiently. Additionally, fennel's ability to calm digestive issues can help to ease headaches and dizziness associated with hangovers, promoting a smoother and more comfortable recovery.
remedy_properties: Digestive aid: Fennel's carminative and anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe an upset stomach, reducing nausea and digestive discomfort caused by excessive alcohol consumption.

Antioxidant properties: Fennel's high antioxidant content helps to neutralize the free radicals produced in the body when it processes alcohol, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to hangover symptoms.

Anxiolytic effects: Fennel's anxiolytic compounds, such as fenchone and anethole, can help to calm nervousness and anxiety caused by hangover, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing the overall discomfort associated with morning after symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots of the fennel plant help alleviate hangover symptoms by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.

Seeds: Containing a unique combination of flavonoids and volatile oils, fennel seeds work to soothe the stomach and intestines, helping to ease nausea and abdominal discomfort commonly associated with hangovers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fennel leaves and flowers from organic sources or store-bought bunches weighing approximately 20-30 grams.
Rinse the fennel plant parts gently under cold running water to remove dirt and debris, then pat dry with paper towels.
Combine 10-15 grams of dried or fresh fennel leaves and flowers in a large teapot or infuser with 1 quart of boiling filtered water.
Steep the herbal mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld together and the medicinal properties to release, then strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Serve the warm fennel tea immediately in demitasses or cups, sweetening with honey or sugar to taste if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with hangover because it contains compounds that help to alleviate dehydration, nausea, and digestive discomfort. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can soothe an upset stomach and calm digestive issues, reducing feelings of queasiness and discomfort. Additionally, licorice root has natural antibacterial and antiviral properties that may help to reduce the severity of hangover symptoms caused by bacterial or viral infections.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the severity of hangover symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and stomach upset.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice tea have been shown to have a protective effect on the liver, which can help mitigate damage caused by excessive alcohol consumption and reduce the risk of hangover-related liver problems.

Isomaltose: Isomaltose is a type of sugar found in licorice root that has been shown to have a stabilizing effect on the digestive system, helping to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with hangovers.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with hangovers.
Bark: Contains flavonoids and saponins that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder measuring 2-3 teaspoons per cup.
Heat filtered water to boiling point and pour over the powder in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes allowing the flavors to meld and the liquor to reach desired strength.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth discarding any solids.
Serve the tea hot or at room temperature enjoying its sweet and slightly bitter flavor.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with hangover because of its natural properties that alleviate nausea, inflammation, and digestive discomfort. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, work to soothe an upset stomach, reducing the risk of vomiting and dehydration. Additionally, ginger's anti-inflammatory effects help to reduce headache and body aches associated with hangovers. Drinking herbal ginger tea can provide relief from these uncomfortable symptoms, allowing you to recover from a night out more quickly and comfortably.
remedy_properties: Digestive Relief: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds help alleviate nausea, cramps, and bloating caused by excessive alcohol consumption, allowing for a more comfortable recovery process.
Antioxidant Properties: Ginger's antioxidant capabilities help neutralize the oxidative stress caused by alcohol metabolism, reducing inflammation and tissue damage in the body.
Vasodilation: Ginger's vasodilatory properties help dilate blood vessels, which can ease headaches, migraines, and dizziness often associated with hangovers.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties and help alleviate nausea and stomach discomfort.
Rhyzomes: Contain high amounts of volatile oils and flavonoids, making them effective in reducing hangover symptoms such as headaches, dizziness, and fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Rinse fresh or dried ginger root under cold running water to remove any impurities.
Peel the ginger root using a vegetable peeler or sharp knife to expose its inner layer.
Slice the peeled ginger into thin rounds or grate it finely using a box grater for optimal release of flavors.
Combine 1 teaspoon of grated fresh ginger with 1 cup boiling water in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard solids before serving hot or chilled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eureka teas helps with hangover because it contains a blend of herbs that work together to alleviate symptoms of a rough morning after. The tea's combination of peppermint, ginger, and lemon balm helps to ease nausea and digestive discomfort, while its caffeine content provides a gentle boost to help recharge and refresh the mind and body. Additionally, the soothing properties of chamomile and hibiscus calm the senses, reducing stress and promoting relaxation as you recover from a wild night out.
remedy_properties: Peppermint Tea: Soothes digestive issues and reduces nausea due to its calming effects on the stomach.
Ginger Tea: Helps alleviate nausea and vomiting by reducing inflammation and spasms in the digestive tract.
Green Tea: Contains antioxidants that help counteract the oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption, as well as caffeine which can aid in rehydration.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in compounds that help to calm the stomach and reduce nausea associated with hangover.
Bark: Contains tannins that aid in detoxification and can help to alleviate symptoms of a hangover.
remedy_recipe: Measure accurately one teaspoon of dried eureka lemon peel for every cup of boiling water needed.
Combine the measured eureka lemon peel with 8 ounces of fresh cold filtered water in a clean glass teapot.
Heat the mixture by placing it over medium heat on a stovetop or in an electric kettle until boiling point is reached.
Remove the teapot from heat source and let the herbal infusion steep for 5 to 7 minutes to allow flavors to combine.
Strain the tea leaves from the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup or teapot.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea teas helps with hangover because of its unique combination of antioxidants, amino acids, and calming properties. The high concentration of catechins in white tea has been shown to help alleviate symptoms such as nausea, headache, and fatigue commonly associated with hangovers. Additionally, the caffeine-free nature of white tea allows for a gentle and soothing rehydration process, helping to replenish lost fluids and electrolytes without exacerbating dehydration.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: White tea contains high levels of antioxidants, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in white tea, such as quercetin, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate hangover symptoms like headaches and fatigue.

Catechins: Catechins, a type of polyphenol found in white tea, have been shown to have antiviral and antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the negative effects of alcohol on the digestive system.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are used because they contain high levels of saponins, which help to neutralize toxins and reduce inflammation in the body.
Bark: Bark is used due to its rich antioxidant content, which helps to combat free radicals and oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
remedy_recipe: Select high-quality camellia sinensis leaves and store them in airtight containers for optimal freshness.
Measure one teaspoon of dried white tea leaves per cup of water and pour boiling water over the leaves.
Steep the mixture for five to seven minutes to allow the flavors and nutrients to infuse fully.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container to remove loose leaves.
Serve the tea immediately, garnished with lemon slices or honey if desired.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat a hangover with herbal teas is by consuming calming blends of chamomile and lemon balm, which soothe the stomach and promote relaxation. Ginger tea is excellent for alleviating nausea, while peppermint tea helps reduce inflammation and improves digestion. Additionally, milk thistle tea can help support liver function and detoxification. Staying hydrated with these herbal teas can replenish essential fluids and electrolytes, ultimately easing hangover symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hangover that are treated with herbal teas include digestive issues, dehydration, and mild anxiety or stress. Peppermint tea can soothe an upset stomach, while chamomile tea can help with relaxation and promoting sleep. Ginger tea is known to alleviate nausea and vomiting, and is also effective in reducing inflammation. For dehydration, rehydrating herbal teas with electrolytes, such as those containing lemon or lime, can help restore balance in the body. Licorice root tea can help with adrenal fatigue and stress-related symptoms. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal teas as a treatment for any ailment.
